Racosperma, now known as Acacia, is a genus of flowering plants in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as the Acacia or wattles. 

The Acacia plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. 

Bioactive constituents of Acacia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

Acacia is commonly prepared as a tea, capsule, or tincture, and is used to treat a range of health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and as a natural remedy for stress and anxiety.
